extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the Stage 20 time trial, exploiting the rolling terrain advantage over lighter specialists while VINGEGAARD extended his second place cushion over CARAPAZ.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT's weight advantage on rolling TT courses versus punchy profiles reveals crucial course selection bias for heavy power riders.
JUMBO-VISMA with only four remaining riders still extracted three stage wins, showing depth-over-numbers can succeed with elite remnants.
POGAČAR soft-pedaled the final TT with 5:45 buffer, signaling Olympics prep prioritization over marginal time gains in decided races.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Distinguished rolling TT terrain preference over punchy first TT, suggesting weight-to-power specialists need course profiling
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Extended second place gap to CARAPAZ from 6 seconds to substantial margin despite depleted team support
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: Consciously throttled effort with commanding GC lead, prioritizing recovery for upcoming Olympics over time maximization
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Failed to replicate past TT form despite maximum visible effort, suggesting declining against-the-clock performance trajectory
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Lost significant time to VINGEGAARD in decisive TT despite minimal starting gap, exposing chronometer weakness for podium defense
